2604.14128.title: Rhetorical Questions in LLM Representations: A Linear Probing Study
2604.14128.paper_summary: Rhetorical questions are asked not to seek information but to persuade or signal stance. How large language models internally represent them remains unclear. We analyze rhetorical questions in LLM representations using linear probes on two social-media datasets with different discourse contexts, and find that rhetorical signals emerge early and are most stably captured by last-token representations. Rhetorical questions are linearly separable from information-seeking questions within datasets, and remain detectable under cross-dataset transfer, reaching AUROC around 0.7-0.8. However, we demonstrate that transferability does not simply imply a shared representation. Probes trained on different datasets produce different rankings when applied to the same target corpus, with overlap among the top-ranked instances often below 0.2. Qualitative analysis shows that these divergences correspond to distinct rhetorical phenomena: some probes capture discourse-level rhetorical stance embedded in extended argumentation, while others emphasize localized, syntax-driven interrogative acts. Together, these findings suggest that rhetorical questions in LLM representations are encoded by multiple linear directions emphasizing different cues, rather than a single shared direction.
2604.14128.paper_summary_zh: 修辭性問題的提出並非為了尋求資訊，而是為了說服或表達立場。大型語言模型如何在內部表示這些問題仍然不清楚。我們使用線性探針分析兩個具有不同話語背景的社交媒體數據集中的修辭性問題，發現修辭信號早期出現，且最穩定地被最後一個標記的表示所捕捉。修辭性問題在數據集中與尋求資訊的問題是線性可分的，並且在跨數據集轉移下仍然可檢測，AUROC達到約0.7-0.8。然而，我們證明可轉移性並不僅僅意味著共享表示。對不同數據集訓練的探針在應用於相同目標語料庫時產生不同的排名，且排名前幾的實例之間的重疊往往低於0.2。質性分析顯示這些差異對應於不同的修辭現象：一些探針捕捉到嵌入於延伸論證中的話語層級修辭立場，而另一些則強調局部的、語法驅動的疑問行為。綜合這些發現表明，在大型語言模型的表示中，修辭性問題是由多個線性方向編碼的，強調不同的線索，而不是單一的共享方向。
2604.14128.author: Louie Hong Yao et.al.
2604.14128.authors: Louie Hong Yao, Vishesh Anand, Yuan Zhuang, Tianyu Jiang
